title: Tom Cruise is utterly transformed in Digger trailer
gist: Ars covers the newly released trailer for Warner Bros.' 'Digger,' Alejandro G. Inarritu's first English-language film since 2015's 'The Revenant,' starring a heavily transformed Tom Cruise as an eccentric billionaire oil baron in an ecological-disaster satire; the film releases in theaters October 2, 2026.

themes.theme: A prestige awards-season bet for Warner Bros.

insights.claim: Digger is director Alejandro G. Inarritu's first English-language film since 2015's The Revenant, an ensemble satire starring a physically transformed Tom Cruise as billionaire oil baron Digger Rockwell, set for theatrical release via Warner Bros. on October 2, 2026, with a cast including John Goodman, Sandra Huller, Jesse Plemons, Riz Ahmed, and Emma D'Arcy.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: A high-profile awards-caliber director's return after a decade-long gap, paired with a transformative lead performance, positions the release as a potential fall 2026 box-office and awards-season catalyst for Warner Bros.' theatrical slate.

insights.claim: Early footage of Digger shown at CinemaCon in April 2026 was reportedly considered a highlight of the event by industry insiders, ahead of the official trailer's release.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Positive early trade buzz ahead of a wide marketing push is a soft leading indicator worth tracking against the film's eventual box-office and critical reception.
insights.locator: When Warner Bros. showed new footage

insights.claim: The film's plot centers on 'the most powerful man in the world' racing to prove he's humanity's savior before an ecological disaster he unleashed (involving a rapidly melting iceberg and nuclear waste) destroys everything, which the author compares to Dr. Strangelove-style political satire updated for contemporary themes.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: A topical, climate/nuclear-themed satire starring Cruise could generate outsized cultural conversation and box-office interest if execution matches the trailer's reception, though this remains speculative ahead of release.
insights.quote: The most powerful man in the world races to prove he's humanity's savior before the disaster he unleashed destroys everything.
insights.locator: The official logline is short and sweet
